game_name: G.I. Joe: A Real American Hero

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy character sprites in distinctive antagonist designs
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring elimination or capture

danger_signals.visual_form: Terrain hazards and non-playable automated weapons in melee levels
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger in current play area

danger_signals.visual_form: Non-playable vehicles and automated weapons shooting at player character
danger_signals.meaning: Incoming damage; need to evade or neutralize

safety_signals.visual_form: Playable character roster from G.I. Joe Special Forces
safety_signals.meaning: Allied unit under player control; safe to assume identity of

safety_signals.visual_form: Overhead view with player-controlled specialized military vehicles
safety_signals.meaning: Increased protection through vehicle armor; distance from melee threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military insignia of United States Special Forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: American military vehicles (Dragonfly helicopter, Skystriker jet, M.O.B.A.T. tank, V.A.M.P. jeep); specialized equipment loadouts for each character

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cobra insignia; distinctive enemy character designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zartan takes form of Joe character (visual deception mechanic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Opposition forces; eight named operatives (Destro, Baroness, Zartan, Firefly, Cobra Commander, Storm Shadow, Major Bludd, Scrap-Iron) visually differentiated from G.I. Joe roster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy character sprites occupy play space; melee combat arena populated with Cobra operatives
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cobra operatives engage in one-on-one combat; non-playable weapons fire at player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overhead vehicular combat sections show player-controlled military vehicles in diverse geographical locations (woods, desert, arctic tundra)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player controls specialized vehicle driver (Ace, Wild Bill, Steeler, or Clutch); combat against Cobra threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Roster status changes when team members placed in jail
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jailed members unavailable for selection; affects team composition; game perpetuates through release of captured allies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Terrain-specific obstacles in three distinct geographic zones (woods, desert, arctic)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental objects impede character movement and create tactical challenges during melee portions

environmental_storytelling.example: Three distinct environmental zones (woods, desert, arctic tundra) communicate operational theater diversity and global threat scope of Cobra Command
Four specialized G.I. Joe vehicles (Dragonfly, Skystriker, M.O.B.A.T., V.A.M.P.) with dedicated pilot characters (Ace, Wild Bill, Steeler, Clutch) establish military hierarchy and specialized roles

Twelve G.I. Joe characters vs. eight Cobra operatives with distinct visual designs; each character has stat-based advantages/disadvantages (speed vs. weapon power) communicating tactical specialization

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Controllable by player; engage in melee combat against Cobra operatives; can be jailed/captured affecting team availability
Engage in one-on-one melee combat; present as capture/elimination targets; Zartan demonstrates enemy deception through character form mimicry
Fire projectiles at player character during melee sections; present as static or semi-mobile obstacles requiring evasion
Removed from active roster; can be released to perpetuate gameplay; status affects available character selection

notes: The 1985 source material describes a game with dual-mode gameplay: overhead vehicular combat and one-on-one melee fighting. Faction identity is strongly communicated through roster differentiation (12 named G.I. Joes vs. 8 named Cobras) and character-specific abilities. Environmental storytelling relies on geographic zone variation and vehicle specialization rather than traditional narrative exposition. The incarceration mechanic creates dynamic world-state communication through roster availability. Zartan's shapeshifting ability introduces visual deception as a strategic element. Character stat distributions (speed vs. power) communicate tactical roles without explicit UI labels.
sources_used: wikipedia - G.I. Joe: A Real American Hero (video game)
